name: logseq-api-mcp
description: Give your AI assistant 22 tools to read, write, query, and semantically search your Logseq knowledge graph — auto-registered from a single Python file drop with no configuration needed.

LOGSEQ_API_TOKEN.description: Bearer auth token from Logseq Settings → Features → Developer mode

LOGSEQ_VERIFY_SSL.description: Set false to skip TLS certificate verification for local/self-signed setups

LOGSEQ_DB_MODE.description: Set true to enable Logseq database-format API paths (SQLite graphs)

LOGSEQ_EXCLUDE_TAGS.description: Comma-separated tags — pages tagged with any of these are hidden from all read operations

LOGSEQ_LOG_LEVEL.description: Log level for the server process (DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR)

LOGSEQ_VECTOR_ENABLED.description: Set true to activate vector_search and vector_db_status tools (requires [vector] extras)

LOGSEQ_VECTOR_PATH.description: LanceDB storage directory for vector search index

LOGSEQ_GRAPH_PATH.description: Root directory of the Logseq markdown graph used by logseq-sync

tools.name: get_all_pages
tools.description: Get a listing of all pages in the Logseq graph with essential metadata.

tools.name: get_page_blocks
tools.description: Get the tree structure of blocks that compose a page in Logseq.

tools.name: get_page_links
tools.description: Get pages that link to the specified page (alias for get_page_backlinks).

tools.name: get_page_backlinks
tools.description: Get pages that link to the specified page with comprehensive metadata.

tools.name: get_block_content
tools.description: Get detailed content and metadata for a specific block using its UUID.

tools.name: get_all_page_content
tools.description: Get comprehensive page content including all blocks and their full content.

tools.name: get_linked_flashcards
tools.description: Get flashcards from the specified page and all pages that link to it.

tools.name: search
tools.description: Search the Logseq graph for matching blocks, pages, and files.

tools.name: query
tools.description: Query the Logseq graph using Datalog DSL syntax.

tools.name: find_pages_by_property
tools.description: Find Logseq pages that have a specific property set.

tools.name: get_pages_from_namespace
tools.description: Get all pages under a Logseq namespace hierarchy.

tools.name: get_pages_tree_from_namespace
tools.description: Get pages under a Logseq namespace displayed as an ASCII tree.

tools.name: create_page
tools.description: Create a new page in Logseq with optional markdown content.

tools.name: delete_page
tools.description: Delete a page from the Logseq graph.

tools.name: rename_page
tools.description: Rename a page in Logseq.

tools.name: update_page
tools.description: Update an existing Logseq page by appending or replacing its content.

tools.name: append_block_in_page
tools.description: Append a new block to a specified page in Logseq.

tools.name: edit_block
tools.description: Edit a block in Logseq.

tools.name: update_block
tools.description: Update the content of a Logseq block.

tools.name: insert_nested_block
tools.description: Insert a new block as a child or sibling of an existing block.

tools.name: delete_block
tools.description: Delete a block from Logseq by its UUID.

tools.name: set_block_properties
tools.description: Set one or more properties on a Logseq block (DB-mode only).

tools.name: vector_search
tools.description: Semantic similarity search across all synced Logseq blocks using LanceDB.

tools.name: vector_db_status
tools.description: Report vector DB status: enabled state, path, document count, and sync state.

vectorSearch.description: Semantic block search via LanceDB. Requires installing the [vector] extra and running logseq-sync.

steps.title: Enable Logseq HTTP API
steps.description: Open Logseq → Settings → Features → Developer mode → Enable HTTP APIs server. Copy the token shown.

steps.title: Set required environment variables
steps.description: Set LOGSEQ_API_TOKEN to the token copied from Logseq. Optionally adjust LOGSEQ_API_ENDPOINT if you changed the port.

steps.title: Verify connection
steps.description: Ask Claude: 'List all my Logseq pages'. You should see your knowledge graph pages returned.
